# UC-22.3.28 · DORA TLPT — Retest and Control Effectiveness Verification Events

## Description

Retesting proves fixes actually work. Correlating retest pass events with original finding IDs closes the assurance loop expected in mature TLPT programs and avoids “fixed on paper” drift.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes tlpt:finding, tlpt:retest. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Require `finding_id` foreign key; (2) block manual status=remediated without retest event; (3) store `evidence_url` out-of-band; (4) integrate with vulnerability scanners for layered proof; (5) auditor sample pack from this search. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tlpt:finding`, `sourcetype=tlpt:retest`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector from attack platform and CI/CD verification jobs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:retest"` (finding_id, retest_id, result, executed_at, evidence_url).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require `finding_id` foreign key; (2) block manual status=remediated without retest event; (3) store `evidence_url` out-of-band; (4) integrate with vulnerability scanners for layered proof; (5) auditor sample pack from this search.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:retest" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(result) as last_result, latest(executed_at) as last_exec by finding_id
| join type=left finding_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:finding" earliest=-400d
    | fields finding_id, status, severity
  ]
| where status="remediated" AND last_result!="pass"
| table finding_id, severity, status, last_result, last_exec
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA TLPT — Retest and Control Effectiveness Verification Events** — Retesting proves fixes actually work. Correlating retest pass events with original finding IDs closes the assurance loop expected in mature TLPT programs and avoids “fixed on paper” drift.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:retest"` (finding_id, retest_id, result, executed_at, evidence_url). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector from attack platform and CI/CD verification jobs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: tlpt:retest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="tlpt:retest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **finding_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where status="remediated" AND last_result!="pass"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: finding_id, last_exec, last_result, severity, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed retests), Timeline, Single value (count).

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:retest" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(result) as last_result, latest(executed_at) as last_exec by finding_id
| join type=left finding_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:finding" earliest=-400d
    | fields finding_id, status, severity
  ]
| where status="remediated" AND last_result!="pass"
| table finding_id, severity, status, last_result, last_exec
```

## Visualization

Table (failed retests), Timeline, Single value (count). Key fields: `finding_id`, `last_exec`, `last_result`, `severity`, `status`.
